Name: SMTP too long line
description: Some antivirus scanners dies when they process an email with a too long string without line breaks. Such a message was sent. If there is an antivirus on your MTA, it might have crashed. Please check its status right now, as it is not possible to do it remotely.
detection_result: Vulnerability was detected according to the Vulnerability Detection Method.
solution: Contact the vendor of the antivirus scanner to get an update.

Name: OpenVAS / Greenbone Vulnerability Manager Default Credentials
description: The remote OpenVAS / Greenbone Vulnerability Manager is installed/configured in a way that it has account(s) with default passwords enabled.
detection_result: It was possible to login using the following credentials (username:password:role): admin:admin:Admin
detection_method: Try to login with default credentials via the OMP/GMP protocol.
product_detection_result: Detected by OpenVAS / Greenbone Vulnerability Manager Detection (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.103825)
impact: This issue may be exploited by a remote attacker to gain access to sensitive information or modify system configuration.
solution: Change the password of the mentioned account(s).
insight: It was possible to login with default credentials: admin/admin, sadmin/changeme, ob-server/observer or admin/openvas.

Name: SSL/TLS: Certificate Expired
description: The remote server's SSL/TLS certificate has already expired.
detection_result: The certificate of the remote service expired on 2020-08-20 19:18:24. Certificate details: subject ...: C=DE,L=Osnabrueck,O=OpenVAS Users,CN=218ffb30ff7a
issued by .: C=DE,L=Osnabrueck,O=OpenVAS Users,OU=Certificate Authority for 218ffb30ff7a
detection_method: This script checks expiry dates of certificates associated with SSL/TLS-enabled services on the target and reports whether any have already expired.
solution: Replace the SSL/TLS certificate by a new one.
insight: This script checks expiry dates of certificates associated with SSL/TLS-enabled services on the target and reports whether any have already expired.

Name: Check if Mailserver answer to VRFY and EXPN requests
description: The Mailserver on this host answers to VRFY and/or EXPN requests.
detection_result: 'VRFY root' produces the following answer: 252 2.0.0 root
detection_method: Details: Check if Mailserver answer to VRFY and EXPN requests
solution: Disable VRFY and/or EXPN on your Mailserver.
For Sendmail add the option 'O PrivacyOptions=goaway'.
It is suggested that, if you really want to publish this type of information, you use a mechanism that legitimate users actually know about, such as Finger or HTTP.
insight: VRFY and EXPN ask the server for information about an address. They are inherently unusable through firewalls, gateways, mail exchangers for part-time hosts, etc.

Name: SSL/TLS: Certicate Expired
description: The remote server's SSL/TLS certicate has already expired.
detection_result: The certificate of the remote service expired on 2020-08-20 19:18:24.
issued by .: C=DE,L=Osnabrueck,O=OpenVAS Users,OU=Certificate Authority for 218ffb30ff7a
solution: Replace the SSL/TLS certicate by a new one.
insight: This script checks expiry dates of certicates associated with SSL/TLS-enabled services on the target and reports whether any have already expired.

Name: SSL/TLS: HTTP Public Key Pinning (HPKP) Missing
description: The remote web server is not enforcing HPKP.
detection_result: The remote web server is not enforcing HPKP.
solution: Enable HPKP or add / congure the required directives correctly following the guides linked in the references.
log_method: Details: SSL/TLS: HTTP Public Key Pinning (HPKP) Missing

Name: SSL/TLS: HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) Missing
description: The remote web server is not enforcing HSTS.
detection_result: The remote web server is not enforcing HSTS.
solution: Enable HSTS or add / congure the required directives correctly following the guides linked in the references.
log_method: Details: SSL/TLS: HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) Missing

log_method: As the NVT 'SSL/TLS: Check Supported Cipher Suites' (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.900234) might run into a timeout the actual reporting of all accepted cipher suites takes place in this NVT instead. The script preference 'Report timeout' allows you to congure if such an timeout is reported.

Name: wapiti (NASL wrapper)
description: This plugin uses wapiti to find web security issues.
Make sure to have wapiti 2.x as wapiti 1.x is not supported.
See the preferences section for wapiti options.
Note that the scanner is using limited set of wapiti options. Therefore, for more complete web assessment, you should use standalone wapiti tool for deeper/customized checks.

Note: The plugin needs the 'wapiti' binary found within the PATH of the user running the scanner and needs to be executable for this user. The existence of this binary is checked and reported separately within 'Availability of scanner helper tools' (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.810000).
detection_result: The wapiti report filename is empty. That could mean that a wrong version of wapiti is used or tmp dir is not accessible. Make sure to have wapiti 2.x as wapiti 1.x is not supported.
In short: Check the installation of wapiti and the scanner.

Name: SMTP antivirus scanner DoS
description: This script sends the 42.zip recursive archive to the mail server. If there is an antivirus filter, it may start eating huge amounts of CPU or memory.
detection_result: For some reason, we could not send the 42.zip file to this MTA.

Name: SMTP Server type and version
description: This detects the SMTP Server's type and version by connecting to the server and processing the buffer received.

The remote SMTP server is announcing the following available ESMTP commands (EHLO response) via an unencrypted connection: 8BITMIME, DSN, ENHANCEDSTATUSCODES, ETRN, PIPELINING, SIZE 10240000, VRFY
log_method: Details: SMTP Server type and version

Name: OpenVAS / Greenbone Vulnerability Manager Detection

Name: Service Detection with '<xml/>' Request
description: This plugin performs service detection.
This plugin is a complement of nd_service.nasl. It sends a '<xml/>' request to the remaining unknown services and tries to identify them.
detection_result: A OpenVAS / Greenbone Vulnerability Manager supporting the OMP/GMP protocol seems to be running on this port.

Name: CPE Inventory
description: This routine uses information collected by other routines about CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.

Name: CPE Inventory
description: This routine uses information collected by other routines about CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.

Name: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting
description: This script consolidates the OS information detected by several NVTs and tries to nd the best matching OS.
Furthermore it reports all previously collected information leading to this best matching OS. It also reports possible additional information which might help to improve the OS detection.
If any of this information is wrong or could be improved please consider to report these to the referenced community portal.
detection_result: Found by NVT: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.108021 (Nmap OS Identification (NASL wrapper ->))
Setting key "Host/runs_unixoide" based on this information
Other OS detections (in order of reliability):
Found by NVT: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.102002 (ICMP based OS Fingerprinting)
Concluded from ICMP based OS fingerprint
log_method: Details: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting

Name: Traceroute
description: A traceroute from the scanning server to the target system was conducted. This traceroute  is provided primarily for informational value only. In the vast majority of cases, it does not  represent a vulnerability. However, if the displayed traceroute contains any private addresses  that should not have been publicly visible, then you have an issue you need to correct.
detection_result: Here is the route from 127.0.0.1 to 127.0.0.1:
solution: Block unwanted packets from escaping your network.
